goal: Find one or more orthogonal specialist signals or conditioning layers that work where the base does not trade, prove additive value through new entries, and pass stability and integration tests.
global_constraints: Base active-signal-v1@1 is immutable — no retuning, threshold changes, or cosmetic variations.
Raw single-filter event entries (H1/H2 style) are permanently forbidden.
Every candidate must be tested in complement-first mode before any integration.
Every prototype must have at least 3 logical layers: new information layer + regime confirmation + density limiter.
Model-first approaches are forbidden until the low-friction wave (stages 3–8) completes.
If a prototype produces hundreds of low-conviction entries, it is rejected — not tuned.
Cannibalism check is mandatory: overlap high + strict-additive weak = automatic rejection.

stages.title: Постмортем plan_v1 и фиксация новых правил
stages.objective: Record a postmortem of the v1 cycle, fix immutable invariants, and enumerate permitted hypothesis families for v2 so the agent cannot repeat failed patterns under new names.
actions: Open a new research_project for the v2 cycle.
Record postmortem: H1/H2 failed on weak selectivity and fees; H3–H5 incomplete due to infrastructure gaps; root cause was shallow candidate architecture, not just wrong families.
Fix v2 invariants: base immutable, no raw H1/H2 retry, complement-first mandatory, single-filter entry banned, model-first banned until low-friction wave done.
Define permitted v2 branches: IV dynamics on iv_est_1, event × state interactions, classifier-transition specialists, cross-symbol leadership (data-gated), model-backed routing (late wave only).
success_criteria: Postmortem recorded in research memory with explicit failure reasons.
v2 invariants and permitted families written to research_map.

stages.title: Построение base-absent research surface
stages.objective: Build an anchor-level table that separates base-fired from base-absent bars, then map missing regimes where the base is silent but the market shows directionality.
actions: Run an experiment to build an anchor-level table: every 1h anchor, base long/short decision, available features, subsequent window outcome for long and short.
Classify each anchor as base_long_fired, base_short_fired, or base_absent.
On base_absent subset, profile by hour, dow, cl_1h/4h/1d, rsi, iv_est, event distances, weak-quarter and weak-month clusters.
Identify subspaces where base is absent but market has directional bias.
Separate subspaces into candidate long specialists, candidate short specialists, and unclear/noisy zones.
success_criteria: Map of missing regimes exists specifically on the base-absent surface with directional bias identified.

stages.title: Wave A — low-friction hypotheses on existing data
stages.objective: Select hypothesis families buildable from already-available 1h data with minimal new custom features, define hypothesis contracts with missing regime and non-duplication rationale.
actions: For family A1 (IV dynamics): design features based on iv_est_1 rate of change, acceleration, sign reversal, relative stretch — not static buckets.
For family A2 (event × state interactions): design structured interactions combining funding/expiry distance with IV dynamics or classifier state, with state confirmation required.
For family A3 (classifier-transition specialists): design features capturing regime transitions between adjacent windows, especially transitions that historically occur where base is absent.
For family A4 (compression-to-release): design non-model features for range compression, low realized mobility, activity decline followed by release with regime confirmation.
For each family write a hypothesis contract: missing regime, why not a duplicate of base, why it should work on base_absent surface.
Cap at 6–8 hypothesis prototypes total.
success_criteria: Small, high-quality list of Wave A hypotheses, each data-ready or nearly data-ready, with explicit non-duplication rationale.

stages.title: Feature contract и публикация custom features для wave A
stages.objective: Translate hypotheses into causally correct features with documented source, lookback, warmup behavior, and human-readable interpretation, then validate and publish.
actions: For each new feature, document: data source, causal availability at decision time, lookback window, warmup behavior, human interpretation.
Implement IV dynamics features: 1h change, 3–6h change, acceleration, reversal, relative-to-recent-median, IV stretch × classifier state.
Implement classifier transition features: state change between adjacent windows.
Implement compression-release features: past-only compression indicators.
For each custom feature: validate, publish, rebuild materialized dataset, inspect materialization.
success_criteria: All Wave A features published, leakage-checked, warmup-verified, and materialized.

stages.title: Hypothesis plausibility на base-absent surface
stages.objective: Perform early triage of hypothesis prototypes specifically on the complement surface of the base, rejecting noisy, too-broad, or single-bucket candidates.
actions: For each prototype, test profitability and slice behavior only on base_absent subset.
Evaluate sample size, stability across hours and dow, check for single-extreme-bucket effects.
Look for structured effects, not just profitable tails.
Reject candidates that produce noise, are too broad, or fail to narrow the space to a meaningful specialist regime.
success_criteria: Only plausibility-approved prototypes remain, each showing structured effect on base-absent surface.

stages.title: Построение strict specialist prototypes
stages.objective: Convert surviving hypotheses into strict multi-layer signal prototypes with at least 3 logical layers, bounded trade density, and documented additive-by-construction rationale.
actions: For each candidate build 2–4 strict prototypes — no wide parameter sweeps.
Each prototype must have: new information layer + regime confirmation + density limiter.
Pre-register for each prototype: expected specialist regime, why additive by construction, expected trade density, why it should not cannibalize base.
Reject any prototype that again produces hundreds of weak entries.
success_criteria: Small set of strict specialist prototypes exists — not a loose grid of weak filters.

stages.title: Complement-first standalone backtests
stages.objective: Run standalone backtests first in complement-only mode (entries only on base_absent anchors), then full standalone, to verify genuine specialist value.
actions: Run each prototype standalone in complement-only mode: signal may only enter on base_absent anchor bars.
Then run full standalone for overall behavior understanding.
Record per-prototype: net PnL, trades, PF, max DD, PnL per trade, top-N trade profit concentration, quarterly profile.
Watch for H1/H2 pattern recurrence: too many trades, PF ≈ 1, fees killing the idea.
success_criteria: Shortlist of prototypes that already look like real specialist candidates on complement-only.

stages.title: Stability, concentration и specialist verification
stages.objective: Verify that surviving prototypes are stable across windows, not concentrated in a single lucky period, and genuinely repair a specific regime where the base was weak or absent.
actions: Run window stability analysis on each complement-approved prototype.
Run condition stability on key conditions.
Identify strong and weak windows.
Check that profit is not carried by a single quarter or anomalous move.
For specialist-type candidates, explicitly prove which regime they fix and that this regime was genuinely weak or empty for the base.
success_criteria: Only stable specialists or honestly bounded reproducible regime-repair layers remain.

stages.title: Интеграция с базой в двух режимах
stages.objective: Test each finalist in strict-additive (candidate banned where base already entered) and permissive-integrated modes, comparing both against the unchanged base.
actions: Run strict-additive integration for each finalist.
Run permissive integration for each finalist.
Compare both modes against unchanged baseline.
Record: delta PnL, delta trades, delta PF, delta DD, weak-quarter repair, strict-vs-permissive gap.
success_criteria: Clear picture of who is genuinely additive vs. who looks good only by mixing with the base.

stages.title: Cannibalization, ownership и new-entry proof
stages.objective: Formally prove that the new layer brings genuinely new trades and new profit, not hidden duplication or base-trade interception.
actions: For each integration candidate compute: overlap with base, truly new trades, displaced base trades, PnL of new trades only, profit ownership.
Check coverage expansion: which hours, states, event subspaces are now covered that were not before.
Apply hard rejection rules: high overlap + weak strict-additive = reject; low new-trade PnL = reject; negative ownership = reject; base interception = reject.
success_criteria: Direct proof exists for each candidate: who genuinely expands the system vs. who parasitizes it.

stages.title: Wave B — medium-friction branch only if Wave A is inconclusive
stages.objective: Explore cross-symbol leadership, model-backed compression/routing, and event-state-model hybrids only if Wave A produced no viable finalists.
actions: Verify Wave A inconclusive before starting any Wave B branch.
B1: Cross-symbol leadership — requires ETHUSDT 1h dataset and cross-symbol custom features.
B2: Model-backed compression/routing — requires models_dataset → models_train → models_to_feature pipeline.
B3: Event-state-model hybrids — late branch, only after B1/B2 have results.
Any Wave B candidate must then pass the same stages 6–10 pipeline as Wave A candidates.
success_criteria: Wave B branches are attempted only with explicit justification; any resulting candidates feed back into the specialist pipeline.
stages.gate_hint: Wave A must be explicitly concluded as inconclusive (no finalists passing stage_10 cannibalism check) before Wave B may start.

stages.title: Walk-forward и финальный отбор
stages.objective: Run walk-forward evaluation on all finalists, compute final uplift vs. base, verify drawdown quality and new-entry proof, and produce the concluding cycle report.
actions: Run walk-forward evaluation for each finalist that passed the cannibalism and ownership checks.
Record walk-forward verdict, final uplift vs. base, DD quality, new-entry proof status.
Write final cycle report with honest assessment of whether a new additive layer was found.

Complete the operational checklist: base untouched, no H1/H2 retries, base-absent surface built, hypotheses genuinely new, complement-first verified, strict-additive and permissive tests done, rejection log filled, cannibalism log filled.
success_criteria: Final report with honest verdict on whether a genuinely additive layer was found.

warnings: Stages 11–12 (Wave B) are intentionally gated behind Wave A inconclusive outcome; agent must not start Wave B unless explicitly justified.
Stage 12 raw_markdown was sparse — objective and actions inferred from surrounding context and the operational checklist.
